title: Reviews & Reputation Agent
shortDescription: Asks the right satisfied clients for a Google review at the right moment, drafts the polished response to every review, and surfaces complaints before they go public.

workflow.label: Pull matter context
workflow.detail: Reviewer matched to past matter if identified

workflow.label: Route for approval
workflow.detail: Your attorney reviews before anything posts

seo.title: AI Reviews & Reputation Agent for Law Firms | Dodonai
seo.description: Ask the right clients for reviews at the right moment, draft polished responses to every public review, and catch complaints before they reach the internet.

hero.headline: AI Reviews & Reputation Agent
hero.subheadline: Identifies the satisfied client at the right moment, drafts the review request, and prepares the firm's response when a review lands. Complaints get surfaced internally before they become a 1-star post.
bullets: Review-request timing tuned to the matter milestone, not a blast cadence
Drafts a personalized request, never a generic template
Polished response to every public review drafted in your voice
Internal complaint surfacing before client posts publicly

scenario.heading: The review you would have asked for too late
paragraphs: A divorce client receives her final decree on a Wednesday. She's relieved, grateful, ready to move on. By the time your firm's quarterly review request goes out 6 weeks later, the emotional moment has passed and the response rate is in the single digits. The Reviews & Reputation agent flags her case as ready for a request the day the decree lands, drafts a personalized note from the lead attorney, and sends it after attorney review. The request lands in her inbox while the gratitude is still fresh.

whatItDoes.heading: What the Reviews & Reputation agent does
bullets: Watches for matter milestones that signal a satisfied client (positive resolution, decree, settlement received)
Drafts a personalized review request from the lead attorney, in your voice
Monitors Google, Avvo, Yelp, and your firm's profile pages for new reviews
Drafts a response to every review (positive or negative) for attorney approval and posting
Surfaces emerging complaint signals from email or client portal messages before they go public

integrations: Google Business Profile API for review monitoring
Avvo, Yelp, and other practice-area review sites
Practice management system for matter milestone signals
Gmail or Outlook for the request and response layer

safety.heading: Safety posture
paragraphs: Requests and responses are drafts under attorney review. The agent never asks for a review from a client whose matter is unresolved or whose interaction history shows friction. Negative-review responses are particularly sensitive; those always route to a partner before posting, with the draft framed for the firm's defamation and ethics posture.

faq.question: What about jurisdictions where attorney review solicitation is restricted?
faq.answer: The agent respects the rules of every jurisdiction your firm practices in. State-specific rules on reviews, testimonials, and solicitation are encoded in skill files and applied per matter venue. If a request is restricted, the agent doesn't draft one.

faq.question: How do you handle the negative review that's actually unfair?
faq.answer: Two steps. First, the agent surfaces the complaint internally so the firm has a chance to address the underlying issue before responding publicly. Second, the public response is drafted to be measured, professional, and respectful of confidentiality. A partner reviews every negative-review response before it posts.

faq.question: Does this work if we don't have a marketing person?
faq.answer: Especially if you don't. The whole point is that the response and request work happens without consuming attorney time or a marketing seat. A partner reviews drafts in passing; the agent handles the rest.
